page: Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 is an x86_64 server instance belonging to the LASv3 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. This configuration features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The CPU cache architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled with 2002 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests indicate average read performance for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks, but write performance for large uncached blocks is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing also demonstrate weaker performance profiles. Conversely, Redis SET operations perform at an average rate of 8.23 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is sustained at 10.03 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L8as_v3 offers a balanced resource density, combining a high memory capacity of 64 GB with a substantial 2002 GB of local storage on an 8-vCPU footprint. This makes it a viable option for workloads requiring high local storage capacity and memory-intensive operations without needing top-tier multi-threaded compute performance. The instance is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers such as Redis, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose storage-heavy workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 is an x86_64 server instance in the LASv3 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, 64.0 GB of memory, and 2002 GB of bundled local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this Premium Storage capable instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, though its multi-core CPU and memory write bandwidth metrics are average to weak. With an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio and substantial local storage, the L8as_v3 provides a balanced resource density for memory and storage-intensive tasks. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers, and storage-heavy workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 is an x86_64 server featuring an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and 2002 GB of local storage, offering strong single-core performance for database and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 is an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 64 GB RAM and 2002 GB local storage, optimized for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered server combining high memory capacity and substantial local storage for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Includes 64.0 GB of system memory and 2002 GB of bundled local storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Exhibits weaker multi-threaded decompression and memory write bandwidth
Optimized for database hosting, caching layers, and storage-heavy tasks
